models.name: Marigold (diffusion depth)
models.blurb: Diffusion-based monocular depth: a Stable-Diffusion-style denoising UNet iteratively refines a depth latent — distinct from single-pass regression depth (Depth Anything / DPT / DepthPro).
models.unlocks: Sharper, detail-preserving monocular depth via a latent-diffusion denoising loop.
models.blockedReason: Marigold is a latent-DIFFUSION pipeline, not a transformers.js model, and transformers.js has no diffusion depth pipeline. Verified 2026-07-19: (1) All prs-eth/marigold-depth-* repos are library:diffusers, safetensors-only, and have NO root config.json (HTTP 404) — only model_index.json declaring a MarigoldDepthPipeline (scheduler + text_encoder + unet + vae components), so pipeline("depth-estimation", "prs-eth/marigold-depth-v1-1") fails at AutoConfig load. (2) transformers.js 3.7.5 AND 4.2.0 register NO marigold class (grep of both dist bundles: zero matches); the depth-estimation pipeline MODEL_FOR_DEPTH_ESTIMATION_MAPPING supports only single-pass regression heads (depth_anything, depth_pro, dpt, glpn, sapiens) — there is no AutoencoderKL / UNet2DConditionModel / DDIMScheduler denoising loop in the library. (3) The one ONNX export (julienkay/Marigold) is a diffusers OnnxStableDiffusionPipeline (model_index.json _class_name:OnnxStableDiffusionPipeline, DDIMScheduler, separate unet/vae_encoder/vae_decoder/text_encoder ONNX) whose UNet alone is 3.46 GB (unet/weights.pb) and requires a hand-written VAE-encode -> iterative DDIM denoise -> VAE-decode loop — not the transformers.js pipeline()/createModelLoader architecture this showcase runs, and not browser-feasible at that size. Not shipped rather than relabel a single-pass regression depth model (Depth Anything / DPT / DepthPro — already built) as diffusion-based Marigold. Unblocks if an onnx-community browser-feasible Marigold export lands AND transformers.js gains a diffusion depth pipeline (or the showcase adds an onnxruntime-web diffusion runtime).

models.name: DepthPro (sharp metric-scale depth)
models.blurb: Apple DepthPro: sharp, boundary-accurate, metric-scale monocular depth — distinct from Depth Anything relative maps. A large WebGPU model.
models.unlocks: Metric-scale distances and clean depth boundaries from one photo — real measurement, crisp cut-outs and AR occlusion — on-device via WebGPU.

models.name: News topic classification (10-class head)
models.blurb: Auto-tag any headline or article onto one of 10 news desks — Sports, Business, Politics, Science… — with a dedicated topic head in one forward pass.
models.unlocks: Instant, private newsroom auto-tagging and topic-routed feeds — a fixed-taxonomy alternative to zero-shot, at ~13 MB and no server round-trip.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: real headless ~13MB download → Business & Finance 99.4% on the Fed headline (49ms WASM), full 10-topic multi-label distribution; cached reload auto-inits with no Load button; desktop(1280) + mobile(360×740 DPR3) responsive-check clean and scrollWidth==clientWidth; 0 console errors; light+dark contrast good. Node cross-check confirmed sigmoid multi-label output (copy corrected from softmax).

models.name: Formality detection (register)
models.blurb: Score any text as formal or informal register — a stylistic axis orthogonal to sentiment — with a live formality meter and word-level register cues.
models.unlocks: Private, in-browser tone checking: keep client emails formal, flag the one casual line in a draft, or paraphrase to shift register — no text leaving the device.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: real headless ~260MB fp32 download (subfolder+model_file_name override) → 99.7% formal on a polite request (correct); Node cross-check confirms informal on casual text; cached reload auto-inits with no Load button. Fixed a 142px mobile overflow from the long model-id token (overflow-wrap:anywhere) → desktop 0 / mobile 9px, responsive-check clean; 0 console errors.

models.name: FinBERT (financial sentiment)
models.blurb: A BERT domain-adapted on a financial corpus and fine-tuned on the Financial PhraseBank: scores finance text as positive / negative / neutral the way a market participant reads it, not a movie-review model.
models.unlocks: Private, on-device triage of headlines, earnings lines and analyst notes by tone — signal not advice — including a neutral class that general sentiment models lack.

models.name: BART-large-MNLI (zero-shot classification)
models.dtypeNote: Ships q4f16 (4-bit weights + fp16 compute, ~309 MB), NOT q8. Verified in-browser 2026-07-19: the q8/int8 build of bart-large-mnli is badly degraded — near-uniform scores, frequently wrong top label (e.g. 'the phone overheats' -> 'billing problem'). q4f16 restores full discrimination (-> 'hardware fault'), matches fp16 quality, runs on WASM, and is smaller than q8. Smallest real working dtype.
models.blurb: THE canonical zero-shot classifier — BART-large fine-tuned on MultiNLI. Classify text against labels you invent, no training.
models.unlocks: Reference-grade intent routing, triage and multi-label tagging with custom labels; contrast a 406M backbone against tiny DeBERTa on the same input to decide what you actually need.

models.name: Keyphrase extraction (token-classification)
models.blurb: Extract the key phrases from a document as highlighted B/I spans — distinct from NER token-classification.
models.unlocks: Auto-tagging, SEO, and document indexing from on-device keyphrase spans.
models.blockedReason: No token-classification keyphrase model ships a browser-loadable ONNX export. Verified 2026-07-19: the KBIR/DistilBERT keyphrase families (Xenova/keyphrase-extraction-kbir-inspec + Xenova/kbir-inspec do not exist → HF 401; ml6team/keyphrase-extraction-kbir-inspec and ml6team/keyphrase-extraction-distilbert-* exist but are PyTorch-only, no onnx/ folder). A scan of 151 keyphrase/keyword repos found ONLY two with any .onnx: gmarcFG/tech-keywords-extractor-onnx and WiseIntelligence/cnmoro-ptt5_...-Optimum-ONNX — both are seq2seq GENERATION models (BART/T5 text2text), NOT token-classification, so they cannot produce the per-token B/I/O tags this page requires. The English one (gmarcFG) was confirmed to load and run in-browser (transformers.js 3.7.5, q8) but its quantized ONNX is degraded to hallucinated garbage output (e.g. 'EEI', 'OOES', 'LORBRIN'; repo ships only quantized ONNX, no higher-precision fallback), and it is architecturally redundant with existing BART/T5 seq2seq pages. Not shipped rather than mislabel a generator or plain NER as keyphrase span extraction. Unblocks when a token-classification keyphrase model (e.g. a KBIR/DistilBERT export via Xenova/onnx-community) ships an ONNX build.

models.name: Bio_ClinicalBERT (clinical fill-mask)
models.blurb: BERT trained on MIMIC-III clinical notes — masked-word prediction with a medical education, contrasted head-to-head with general BERT.
models.unlocks: Private, on-device clinical autocomplete, term/abbreviation scoring, and a measurable domain-vs-general gap — no prompt engineering, no API bill.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: real download+run in headless Chrome (fresh profile → Download → ready; 'acute [MASK] failure' → respiratory 95.4% logit 17.02). responsive-check desktop+mobile automated-clean (1280x800 + 360x740 DPR3); dark desktop and light mobile screenshots read (no overflow; clinical-vs-general comparison grid collapses to 1 column under 560px; prediction logit column hidden on narrow). All 4 ladder pages load with 0 console errors, 0 net failures, 0 overflow on both classes. WASM path (no GPU needed).

models.name: Legal-BERT (legal fill-mask)
models.blurb: Legal-domain masked LM — [MASK] completion on contract/statute text, contrasted head-to-head with general BERT. Distinct legal label domain vs the general/clinical fill-mask.
models.unlocks: Private, on-device legal-clause autocomplete and a measurable legal-vs-general domain gap.
models.blockedReason: No legal-domain fill-mask model ships a browser-loadable ONNX with a working masked-LM head. Verified 2026-07-19 (transformers.js 3.7.5, q8/fp32, headless Chrome WASM): (1) Xenova/legal-bert-base-uncased does not exist (HF 401). (2) The canonical Legal-BERT fill-mask models are PyTorch-only, no ONNX export: nlpaueb/legal-bert-base-uncased, nlpaueb/legal-bert-small-uncased, pile-of-law/legalbert-large-1.7M-2, casehold/legalbert, law-ai/InLegalBERT. (3) The only legal-BERT ONNX repos with a transformers.js standard layout are broken as masked LMs: adalbianco/legal_bert_onnx exports a BertForPreTraining graph whose sole output tensor is the next-sentence head — AutoModelForMaskedLM returns logits of shape [1,2] (no vocab dimension), and the fill-mask pipeline throws 'IndexError: index N out of bounds for dimension with size 2'; aazharnaeem/legal-bert-onnx returns a logits output that is not a usable tensor (accessing .dims throws; no vocab logits). (4) philGyamfi/legalbert.onnx has no config.json (HF 404) so transformers.js cannot determine the model type and will not load. (5) sekarkrishna/legalbert-int8 is a feature-extraction export (no MLM head). No onnx-community or Xenova legal-domain fill-mask export exists (pipeline_tag=fill-mask + onnx filter returns empty). Feature-extraction-only / NSP-only exports are NOT fill-mask. Not shipped rather than mislabel general BERT or a broken head as Legal-BERT. Unblocks when a legal-domain fill-mask model ships a browser-loadable ONNX with a masked-LM (prediction) head (e.g. a Xenova/onnx-community export of nlpaueb/legal-bert-base-uncased).

models.name: SciBERT (scientific fill-mask)
models.blurb: SciBERT's scientific-vocabulary masked LM — distinct from general BERT via its 31k scivocab tokenizer.
models.unlocks: Scientific-domain masked-word prediction with a science-native vocabulary.
models.blockedReason: No SciBERT scivocab masked-LM (fill-mask) runs in the browser today. Verified 2026-07-19 (transformers.js 3.7.5, q8, headless Chrome): allenai/scibert_scivocab_uncased ships no ONNX (PyTorch-only). The only SciBERT scivocab ONNX exports — Xenova/scibert_scivocab_uncased and onnx-community/scibert_scivocab_uncased-ONNX — are FEATURE-EXTRACTION exports with no masked-LM head: pipeline('fill-mask', ...) throws "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '0')" because the model emits last_hidden_state, not vocab logits (config architectures:null; the scivocab vocab_size 31090 confirms it is the SciBERT encoder without the cls/predictions head). The nearest biomedical fill-mask ONNX that DOES ship an MLM head, onnx-community/biobert_v1.1_pubmed-ONNX (BertForMaskedLM), loads but its head is broken — a near-uniform distribution (top prob ~0.3-0.5%, tokens like 'coordinate','##bal') at BOTH q8 and fp32, so it is unusable, not a quantisation artifact. The domain fill-mask slot is instead served by the runnable, verified onnx-community/Bio_ClinicalBERT-ONNX (slug bio-clinicalbert). Unblocks when a SciBERT scivocab model ships a browser-loadable ONNX WITH a masked-LM head.

models.name: Biomedical NER (biomedical-ner-all)
models.blurb: Tag 40+ biomedical entity types — diseases, medications, dosages, lab values, procedures, anatomy — in clinical text, inline with per-token BIO tags. Distinct from general/multilingual NER by its domain label set.
models.unlocks: On-device clinical literature mining, first-pass note structuring, and de-identification with nothing leaving the tab.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: driven headless-Chrome run (fresh profile → Download → ready; cardiology sample → 16 entities/11 types/116 ms WASM; e.g. inferior myocardial infarction→Disease_disorder, aspirin→Medication, 300mg→Dosage, 63-year-old→Age). Cached RELOAD auto-inits to ready with no Download button. responsive-check desktop+mobile automated-clean (1280x800 + 360x740 DPR3); driven mobile no-horizontal-overflow=true. Screenshots read: desktop light+dark and mobile light — inline entity tags wrap, at-a-glance table stacks, 8-group legend + count bars reflow, WCAG-AA entity colours in both themes, no overflow, no clipped controls. 0 console errors, 0 network failures across download+reload. WASM path (no GPU needed).

mobile: 4 sub-24px target(s) — agent to verify tap size

models.name: Emotion — 7-class Ekman (DistilRoBERTa)
models.blurb: Pick one of the 7 Ekman emotions — anger, disgust, fear, joy, neutral, sadness, surprise — with a real softmax that names a single winner.
models.unlocks: Decisive, private emotion labels in the browser: one-tap message triage, single-emotion mood tags, and line-by-line story/lyric arcs, with a see-inside emotion wheel and nothing uploaded.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: headless Chrome real inference (winner "surprise" 76ms WASM), cached auto-init verified, desktop 1280×800 + mobile 360×740 screenshots read — no horizontal overflow, legible, emotion wheel + bars render, links wrap; light+dark checked.

models.name: Irony / sarcasm detection (Twitter-RoBERTa)
models.blurb: Decide whether text is ironic/sarcastic — where the literal words and intended meaning diverge — with a Twitter-RoBERTa classifier.
models.unlocks: A cheap on-device sarcasm guardrail: flag ironic text before a sentiment model trusts it, screen moderation edge cases, and chain irony + sentiment to recover the writer’s true meaning.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: headless Chrome real inference ("ironic" 55ms WASM), cached auto-init verified, desktop 1280×800 + mobile 360×740 screenshots read — no horizontal overflow, legible, irony meter + prob bars render, links wrap; light+dark checked.

models.name: Silero VAD (voice activity detection)
models.blurb: Find the speech in any audio — a per-frame speech/non-speech probability from a tiny ~2 MB recurrent net, via onnxruntime-web.
models.unlocks: On-device voice activity detection: cut ASR cost by skipping silence, endpoint voice UIs, trim/segment recordings, and build live talk-meters — no server, no per-call bill.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: read desktop(1280x800)+mobile(360x740 DPR3) screenshots (reports/responsive/screens/silero-vad/) — no horizontal overflow/clipping, legible, controls operable. Full headless flow: Download → real output (4 speech segments, 83ms, onnxruntime-web) → reload auto-init, console/network clean.

models.name: Speech Emotion Recognition (wav2vec2)
models.blurb: Hear the feeling, not the words — emotion from the VOICE (angry/happy/sad/fearful/disgust/neutral) with confidence bars, on-device.
models.unlocks: On-device affective audio: call-centre distress triage, spoken UX-feedback sentiment, and expressive interfaces — with the voice never leaving the browser.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: read desktop(1280x800)+mobile(360x740 DPR3) screenshots (reports/responsive/screens/speech-emotion-recognition/) — no overflow/clipping, legible, two-column multi-model collapses to one on mobile. Full headless flow: Download → real on-page output (Disgust 64% + full 6-class bars, 2787ms) → console clean.

models.unlocks: Private, offline code assistance — generation, completion, tests, and explanation — with no snippet ever leaving the device and no API bill.

automatedNotes: desktop: 10 sub-24px target(s) — agent to verify tap size
mobile: 4 sub-24px target(s) — agent to verify tap size

models.name: Mistral 7B Instruct v0.3 (WebLLM chat)
models.blurb: Mistral 7B Instruct v0.3 — the catalogue's most capable model — running on MLC's WebGPU engine, fully local. A heavy ~4 GB download.
models.unlocks: Server-grade private chat with no server: on-device 7B reasoning, tool-style prompting, personas, and grounded RAG with no API bill, for devices with a capable GPU.

automatedNotes: desktop: 13 sub-24px target(s) — agent to verify tap size
mobile: 7 sub-24px target(s) — agent to verify tap size

models.name: ConvNeXt (image classification)
models.blurb: A pure ConvNet that matches transformers on ImageNet — with a ConvNeXt-vs-ViT head-to-head on the same image.
models.unlocks: A stronger on-device tagger and cheap architecture ensembling (ConvNet + transformer) for robustness in the browser.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass (headless Chrome): fresh profile → Download (~29 MB) → auto-init ready → Classify cats sample = tabby 67.9%, tiger cat 20.9% (real ImageNet labels), 226 ms WASM, 1000 classes. Cached reload auto-inits with NO Download button; all 4 sub-pages auto-init clean. Desktop 1280×800 (dark) + mobile 360×740 DPR3 reviewed: no horizontal overflow, at-a-glance table wraps, legible, chips ≥40px; responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; 0 console errors.

models.name: ResNet-50 (image classification)
models.blurb: The original 2015 deep residual CNN: name what is in an image across 1,000 ImageNet classes — with a ResNet-vs-ConvNeXt-vs-ViT backbone lineage race.
models.unlocks: A dependable, battle-tested on-device tagger and a concrete architecture baseline (2015 CNN vs 2022 ConvNeXt vs ViT) in the browser.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass (headless Chrome): fresh profile → Download (~26 MB, no auto-download) → auto-init ready → Classify cats sample = tiger cat 96.2%, tabby 1.3%, sock 0.6% (real ImageNet labels), 176 ms WASM, 1000 classes. Cached reload (new tab, same profile) auto-inits to ready with NO Download button. Desktop 1280×800 (light) + dark + mobile 360×740 DPR3 screenshots reviewed: no horizontal overflow (0px on mobile), at-a-glance table wraps, legible, chips ≥40px, versus-grid stacks 3→1 on narrow; responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; 0 console errors.

models.name: BEiT (image classification)
models.blurb: BERT for images: a Vision Transformer pretrained self-supervised by masking image patches — with a BEiT-vs-supervised-ViT head-to-head.
models.unlocks: Label-efficient, self-supervised image features and a different-error second opinion vs the supervised ViT, in the browser.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass (headless Chrome): fresh profile → Download (~110 MB, no auto-download) → auto-init ready → Classify cats sample = Egyptian cat 55.2%, tabby 26.4%, tiger cat 6.5% (real ImageNet labels), 514 ms WASM, 1000 classes. Cached reload (new tab, same profile) auto-inits to ready with NO Download button. All 4 sub-pages (basics/practical/wild/multimodel) load with 0 console errors; the wild patch-mask canvas renders correctly (19968 grey masked px at 40%). Desktop 1280×800 (light) + dark + mobile 360×740 DPR3 screenshots reviewed: no horizontal overflow (0px on mobile), at-a-glance table wraps, legible, chips ≥40px, versus-grid stacks on narrow; responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; 0 console errors.

models.name: MobileNetV2 (image classification)
models.blurb: A ~3.5M-param mobile backbone: name what is in an image fast, with a real latency read-out and live webcam tagging.
models.unlocks: Real-time on-device camera tagging and a cheap fast-prefilter → heavy-confirm cascade in the browser.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass (headless Chrome): fresh profile → Download (~14 MB) → auto-init ready → Classify cats sample = tabby 71.1%, tiger cat 25.9% (real ImageNet labels), 40 ms WASM, 1001 classes (background class confirmed). Cached reload auto-inits with NO Download button; all 4 sub-pages auto-init clean. models.name: Food-101 (dish classification)
models.blurb: Name the dish across 101 Food-101 classes — a specialised food classifier with a confidence read-out.
models.unlocks: Private, offline menu & nutrition tagging, food-diary logging, and dish de-duplication in the browser.

models.name: Places365 scene classification
models.blurb: Classify a photo's scene/place (kitchen, beach, office, …) across the 365 Places365 categories — a scene classifier, distinct from object/ImageNet classification.
models.unlocks: Photo-library auto-organisation by place, indoor/outdoor gating, and context-aware apps from an on-device scene label.
models.blockedReason: No Places365 scene classifier runs in the browser via transformers.js today. Verified 2026-07-19 (transformers.js 3.7.5, image-classification): there is no ONNX export with a config.json/preprocessor_config.json that transformers.js can load. Scanned the Places365/scene/indoor repos on HF: corenet-community/places365-224x224-vit-{base,large,huge} ship ONLY a raw PyTorch checkpoint (vit_base.pt) with no config.json and no ONNX; pratap4dev/resnet50-places365 and birder-project/rope_vit_reg4_b14_capi-places365 have no config.json and no ONNX (birder-native weights); litert-community/Places365-ResNet18-LiteRT ships ONLY places_fp16.tflite — a bare TFLite tensor graph, not a transformers.js ONNX and not a MediaPipe .task ImageClassifier bundle with embedded label metadata. The HF `onnx` tag filter returns no Places365/scene/indoor image classifier. The canonical MIT Places365 weights are Caffe/PyTorch only. Not shipped rather than relabel a generic ImageNet ViT as a scene classifier (ImageNet's 1,000 classes are objects, not the 365 scene categories). Unblocks when a Places365 model ships a browser-loadable ONNX (onnx-community/Xenova export with config + preprocessor), or when a metadata-bearing Places365 .tflite/.task is adopted via the MediaPipe ImageClassifier path.

models.name: GOT-OCR-2.0
models.blurb: GOT-OCR-2.0 is a unified end-to-end OCR-2.0 model: whole-page dense/document/scene text, formatted (markdown/LaTeX) output. Distinct from TrOCR's single-line reader. Not runnable in the browser today — see blockedReason.
models.unlocks: Whole-page structured OCR (document/receipt/screenshot/whiteboard → formatted text) — if it ran in-browser. Blocked by both a missing ONNX export and a runtime-class gap, not a weights-license gap.
models.blockedReason: GOT-OCR-2.0 does not run via the shared Transformers.js path today (verified 2026-07-19), for three independent reasons. (1) NO ONNX export exists: onnx-community/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf returns HTTP 401/absent and the onnx-community org has no GOT repo; the canonical stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR2_0 (model_type 'GOT', arch GOTQwenForCausalLM) and stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf (model_type 'got_ocr2', arch GotOcr2ForConditionalGeneration) ship only model.safetensors — no onnx/ folder. (2) The pinned Transformers.js 3.7.5 (webai.js TRANSFORMERS_URL) does not register the 'image-text-to-text' pipeline task at all: pipeline('image-text-to-text', ...) → 'Unsupported pipeline: image-text-to-text. Must be one of [...]'. (3) NO got_ocr2 model class is registered in Transformers.js 3.7.5 OR 4.2.0 (latest) — got_ocr2 is absent from both MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES and MODEL_FOR_VISION_2_SEQ_MAPPING_NAMES — so even an -hf ONNX (if one existed) would throw 'Unsupported model type: got_ocr2'. Running GOT would require a bespoke ONNX Runtime Web pipeline + hand-wired Qwen tokenizer/generation loop off the shared createModelLoader path (inv12 / SKILL 4b). Recheck when an ONNX export ships AND Transformers.js adds a got_ocr2 class.

models.name: GIT — Generative Image-to-Text
models.blurb: GIT (GenerativeImage2Text) is a single generative transformer conditioned on image patches — captioning + VQA in one architecture, distinct from ViT-GPT2/BLIP encoder-decoders. Not runnable in the browser today — see blockedReason.
models.unlocks: Image → generated caption (and VQA) from one unified transformer — if it ran in-browser. Blocked by both a missing ONNX export and a runtime-class gap.
models.blockedReason: GIT (Microsoft GenerativeImage2Text; config model_type 'git', arch GitForCausalLM) does not run in the browser today (verified 2026-07-19). (1) NO ONNX export exists anywhere on HF: Xenova/git-base-coco and Xenova/git-base-vqav2 do not exist (HTTP 401 absent — Transformers.js load → 'Unauthorized access to file: .../Xenova/git-base-coco/resolve/main/config.json'); an HF search for browser-runnable git-base repos returns only PyTorch/flax checkpoints, none with an onnx/ folder. (2) The pinned Transformers.js 3.7.5 (and 4.2.0 latest) registers NO 'git' model class: 'git' is absent from MODEL_FOR_VISION_2_SEQ_MAPPING_NAMES, so pipeline('image-to-text', 'microsoft/git-base-coco') → 'Unsupported model type: git'. GIT's image-patch-conditioned single-transformer generation is architecturally distinct from ViT-GPT2 (built, VisionEncoderDecoder) and BLIP, but neither a runnable ONNX export nor a Transformers.js class exists. Recheck when an ONNX git export ships AND Transformers.js adds a GitForCausalLM class.

models.name: PEGASUS XSum (summarization)
models.blurb: PEGASUS is the purpose-built abstractive-summarization architecture: its Gap-Sentence Generation pretraining masks whole salient sentences and makes the model regenerate them. XSum is the extreme single-sentence variant. Not runnable in the browser today — see blockedReason.
models.unlocks: Extreme single-sentence abstractive summaries — if it ran in-browser. Blocked by a runtime gap, not a weights gap.
models.blockedReason: No transformers.js support for the PEGASUS architecture in ANY version (verified 3.7.5, 4.0.0, 4.2.0-latest): 'Unsupported model type: pegasus'. Real canonical ONNX exports DO exist (onnx-community/pegasus_paraphrase-ONNX ships a full onnx/ folder with encoder + merged decoder across all dtypes; Rahmat82/pegasus_dialogue_summarization_onnx_quantized ships quantized encoder/decoder) but transformers.js registers no PegasusForConditionalGeneration / PegasusTokenizer, so both fail to instantiate. Running PEGASUS would require a bespoke ONNX Runtime Web pipeline + hand-wired SentencePiece tokenizer + generation loop, off the shared createModelLoader path (invariant 12 / SKILL 4b). Distinct from LongT5 (built), which is browser-runnable and reuses PEGASUS-style gap-sentence pretraining on a T5 backbone. Recheck when transformers.js adds a pegasus model class.

models.blockedReason: Not browser-runnable (2026-07-19): duplicate of the blocked got-ocr2 family. No transformers.js class for model_type got_ocr2 in 3.7.5/4.2 and the image-text-to-text pipeline task does not exist in 3.7.5; stepfun-ai repos ship safetensors only (no onnx/). Re-check when transformers.js registers a GOT-OCR class.

models.name: Multilingual MiniLM (cross-lingual embeddings)
models.blurb: One 384-d embedding space shared across 50+ languages: a sentence and its translation land at nearly the same point, so cosine similarity measures meaning, not language.
models.unlocks: Cross-language search, dedup and clustering from a single index — an English query finds its French or Japanese answer, with no per-language model and no translation round-trip.

automatedNotes: desktop: 13 sub-24px target(s) — agent to verify tap size
mobile: 6 sub-24px target(s) — agent to verify tap size

models.name: BGE-M3 (100-language dense embeddings)
models.blurb: The dense-retrieval head of BAAI’s BGE-M3: one 1024-d space shared across 100+ languages with an 8192-token context window, so it embeds whole documents in any language. Larger than the other embedders (~570 MB q8) — the trade for breadth, length and quality.
models.unlocks: Multilingual RAG and long-document search from a single on-device index — a query in any language finds the right passage in any other, no per-language model and no translation round-trip.

automatedNotes: desktop: 13 sub-24px target(s) — agent to verify tap size
mobile: 6 sub-24px target(s) — agent to verify tap size

models.name: RT-DETRv2 (improved real-time detection)
models.blurb: The improved real-time DETR: a flexible, discrete-sampling decoder gives cleaner boxes at v1's speed and size.
models.unlocks: A drop-in upgrade for real-time detection — better localisation at the same latency and ~21 MB — with a fair v2-vs-v1 race, all on-device.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: real download+run in headless Chrome (fresh profile → Download → ready; cat 97.0%/remote 96.6%/sofa 87.7% boxes on the cats sample). responsive-check desktop+mobile automated-clean (1280x800 + 360x740 DPR3); light+dark desktop and mobile screenshots read (no horizontal overflow, legible text, at-a-glance table wraps, canvas scales). All 4 ladder pages load with 0 console errors, 0 net failures, 0 overflow on both classes. WASM path (no GPU needed).

models.name: ViLT (visual question answering)
models.blurb: Patch+text co-transformer VQA — no ONNX build runs in the browser today.
models.unlocks: Would answer free-text questions about an image with no external object detector.
models.blockedReason: Not runnable in transformers.js 3.7.5 (verified 2026-07-19). Three compounding blockers: (1) transformers.js registers no `vilt` model class and `visual-question-answering` is not a supported pipeline task (`Unsupported pipeline: visual-question-answering`); (2) its processor is unimplemented (`Unknown feature_extractor_type: 'ViltFeatureExtractor'`); (3) no ONNX export exists — `Xenova/vilt-b32-finetuned-vqa` does not exist and `dandelin/vilt-b32-finetuned-vqa` ships only PyTorch weights (no `onnx/model_quantized.onnx`). Unblock: an upstream transformers.js ViltForQuestionAnswering class + processor AND a published ONNX export. Donut (document-question-answering) already covers browser VQA for documents.

models.name: GLiNER (zero-shot NER by arbitrary labels)
models.blurb: Generalist zero-shot NER: extract spans for entity types you invent at inference time.
models.unlocks: Would enable user-defined-schema extraction (resume/invoice/contract fields, domain entities) with no fixed label set — if a browser runtime existed.
models.blockedReason: transformers.js 3.7.5 registers NO `gliner` model class: pipeline('token-classification','onnx-community/gliner_small-v2.1') throws 'Unsupported model type: gliner', and the CDN bundle contains zero 'gliner' references. The onnx-community repos ship an ONNX export + config.json {model_type:'gliner'} but GLiNER's bidirectional span/entity-prompt pre/post-processing is not implemented as a transformers.js class (same for onnx-community/gliner-multitask-large-v0.5). Verified 2026-07-19 in headless Chrome. Unblocks when transformers.js adds a GLiNER model class/processor.

models.name: DETR panoptic (segmentation)
models.blurb: Panoptic segmentation: split a scene into per-instance object masks (things) plus labelled background regions (stuff), each colour-coded.
models.unlocks: On-device instance-accurate cut-outs, scene inventory, and region-level redaction with nothing uploaded.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: real inference verified in headless Chrome (WASM), no horizontal overflow / clipped controls on 1280x800 + 360x740, clean console + network, legible text, screenshots reviewed.

models.name: MobileCLIP (zero-shot image)
models.blurb: Apple MobileCLIP-S0: fast on-device zero-shot image classification — rank an image against any labels, with a mobile-tuned vision tower.
models.unlocks: Fast on-device tagging, moderation triage, and real-time camera classification at a fraction of a full ViT-B/16 CLIP encode time.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: real inference verified in headless Chrome (WASM), no horizontal overflow / clipped controls on 1280x800 + 360x740, clean console + network, legible text, screenshots reviewed.

models.name: Facial expression recognition
models.blurb: Read a facial expression on-device across 7 emotions — from your camera or a photo — with a confidence + calibration read-out.
models.unlocks: Private, consented affective UX in the browser: reaction capture, sentiment-aware flows, and expression-over-time — with the camera frame never leaving the device.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: headless real inference (sample face → happy 97%, WASM, 0 console errors, auto-init on cached reload with no Load button); desktop+mobile responsive screenshots read — no horizontal overflow, legible text, controls stack and are keyboard-operable. Camera path degrades honestly when getUserMedia is unavailable.

models.name: Spoken language identification (MMS-LID)
models.blurb: Identify the spoken language of audio out of 126 — from the mic, a file, or a sample — the audio-first cousin of text language detection.
models.unlocks: The router step before every multilingual speech pipeline: detect the language on-device, then send the audio to the right ASR / translator with no server round-trip.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: headless real inference (English clip → eng 94%, entropy 0.090, WASM, 0 console errors, auto-init on cached reload with no Load button); desktop+mobile responsive screenshots read — no horizontal overflow, legible, controls stack. Mobile note: model is a large ~974 MB one-time download (surfaced in at-a-glance); UI/controls fully usable on mobile with the download cost disclosed — best on desktop / good Wi-Fi.

models.name: YOLOv10 (NMS-free detection)
models.blurb: Real-time object detection with no Non-Max-Suppression — one pass returns already-deduplicated boxes.
models.unlocks: Fast, tiny on-device detection for live camera, counting and inventory, with nothing uploaded.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass 2026-07-19: headless Chrome real download+detect (2 cats/2 remotes/1 couch on cats sample, 213ms WASM), multi-model crop→ViT verified; desktop 1280 light+dark + mobile 360x740 DPR3 screenshots reviewed — no overflow, legible, operable controls, 0 console errors.

models.name: XLM-RoBERTa (multilingual fill-mask)
models.blurb: Fill a <mask> in ~100 languages with one model sharing a single 250k-token vocabulary.
models.unlocks: One on-device model that serves multilingual autocomplete, knowledge probing and cloze tasks worldwide.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass 2026-07-19: headless Chrome real 280MB download + auto-run (Paris→France 30.5%, multilingual DE/ES/JA verified, 392ms WASM); desktop 1280 light + mobile 360x740 DPR3 screenshots reviewed — no overflow, legible, <mask> renders, 0 console errors.

models.name: OuteTTS (LLM text-to-speech)
models.blurb: LLM-based TTS — a Qwen2 model generates discrete audio tokens that the WavTokenizer neural codec decodes into speech.
models.unlocks: Private, controllable narration where the same sampling knobs as a chat LLM shape the voice — no API keys, no per-character billing.

automatedNotes: Responsive matrix CLEAN on both classes: desktop 1280x800 and mobile 360x740 — overflow=0, clipped=0, console+network clean; screenshots read and confirmed legible (reports/responsive/screens/outetts/{desktop,mobile}.png).
Live headless-Chrome run showed the honest first-visit state (Download model (~580 MB) button; no silent download).
Runnability PROVEN via Node transformers.js: OuteTTS-0.2-500M + WavTokenizer generated 39040 non-silent samples (24 kHz, ~1.6 s, peak 0.67) for a real phrase.

Full in-browser generation NOT agent-confirmed here: the ~580 MB q8 model on the WASM path exceeds the memory-constrained headless runner (renderer unresponsive). Needs a capable device (WebGPU or more RAM) — same posture as the sibling kokoro-text-to-speech (needs-review). Agent to confirm audio on a real device.

models.name: ViTPose (pose estimation)
models.blurb: A plain Vision Transformer that estimates 17 COCO body keypoints from per-joint heatmaps — a top-down pose estimator, distinct from MediaPipe PoseLandmarker.
models.unlocks: On-device human pose estimation with a transformer: fitness form checks, joint-angle analysis, stick-figure puppets, and detector→pose for whole scenes — no depth camera, no cloud.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: real inference verified in headless Chrome (WASM) — 17/17 COCO keypoints on a person, 525ms; multi-model detector→ViTPose estimated 8 poses. No horizontal overflow / clipped controls at 1280x800 + 360x740, clean console + network, legible text, screenshots reviewed. Primary controls full-size; sample thumbs 76x56.

models.name: Multilingual zero-shot image (Jina-CLIP v2)
models.blurb: Match an image to text labels written in ANY of 89 languages — the same concept in English, Spanish, Chinese, or Arabic scores alike. (AltCLIP has no Transformers.js class; jina-clip-v2 delivers the capability.)
models.unlocks: Global, on-device image tagging, moderation, and cross-lingual search with one model instead of one per locale.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: real inference verified in headless Chrome (WASM, ~874MB q8) — French label "chats endormis" correctly ranked #1 on a cats image (cross-lingual), 7 labels scored, 14.4s. No horizontal overflow / clipped controls at 1280x800 + 360x740 (RTL Arabic + CJK render), clean console + network, screenshots reviewed.

models.name: Qwen2.5 0.5B on the CPU (WASM)
models.blurb: A capable 0.5B instruct LLM streaming on the CPU via WebAssembly — no WebGPU required.
models.unlocks: Client-side chat, extraction, and RAG on ANY device with a WASM engine — no GPU, no server, no key.

support.notes: Agent matrix pass (headless Chrome, fresh profile): main page driven end-to-end on desktop — Download → real streamed output ("Three primary colors are red, green, and blue.", WASM q8, 8 tok @ 5.3 tok/s, 1st token 663ms), see-inside top-k rendered 12 rows, warm-cache reload auto-inits to ready with only a Clear-cache button (no Download). All 5 pages: zero console errors, zero failed network requests, no horizontal overflow at 1280x800 and 360x740 (DPR3, touch). Light + dark screenshots read and confirmed against the design system. Primary controls (textareas, Send/Ask/Extract) >=44px; example chips 40px.

models.name: Kosmos-2 (grounded image-to-text)
models.blurb: Kosmos-2 is a grounded VLM: it generates a caption AND grounds noun phrases to bounding boxes. Not runnable in the browser today — see blockedReason.
models.unlocks: Would enable grounded captioning + referring-expression localization (describe-and-locate, a11y alt-text with regions) — if a browser runtime existed. Blocked by both a missing ONNX export and a runtime-class gap.
models.blockedReason: Kosmos-2 (config model_type kosmos-2, arch Kosmos2ForConditionalGeneration) does not run in the browser today (verified 2026-07-19). (1) NO transformers.js model class exists in ANY version: mod.Kosmos2ForConditionalGeneration is undefined in the pinned 3.7.5, and the CDN dist bundles for 3.7.5, 4.2.0 and latest contain ZERO kosmos references (grep), while florence2/sam2/idefics/smolvlm ARE present — so pipeline("image-to-text", "microsoft/kosmos-2-patch14-224") maps to no class. (2) NO browser-loadable ONNX export exists: Xenova/kosmos-2-patch14-224 does not exist (HF 401 -> transformers.js load throws "Unauthorized access to file: .../tokenizer_config.json"); the canonical microsoft/kosmos-2-patch14-224 ships only model.safetensors + pytorch_model.bin (no onnx/ folder); no onnx-community Kosmos-2 export exists. Kosmos-2s grounded caption-plus-boxes output is architecturally distinct from Florence-2 (built, WebGPU) and BLIP, but neither a runnable ONNX export nor a transformers.js class exists. Recheck when an ONNX Kosmos-2 export ships AND transformers.js registers a Kosmos2ForConditionalGeneration class.

models.name: ByT5-small (byte-level seq2seq)
models.blurb: A token-free byte-level seq2seq model: it reads raw UTF-8 bytes instead of sub-word tokens, so every script, emoji, and typo is handled identically and nothing is ever out-of-vocabulary.
models.unlocks: Truly multilingual, noise-robust text processing with no vocabulary to ship — plus a legible demo of how tokenization actually works.

automatedNotes: desktop: 11 sub-24px target(s) — agent to verify tap size
mobile: 4 sub-24px target(s) — agent to verify tap size

models.name: InternVL2.5-1B (vision-language)
models.blurb: A compact InternVL vision-language model (ViT vision encoder + pixel-shuffle projector + InternLM/Qwen2 decoder) for image question-answering and document reasoning.
models.unlocks: Would add a distinct on-device VLM family (alongside Qwen2-VL / PaliGemma / SmolVLM / Moondream / nanoLLaVA) — but is not browser-runnable today.
models.blockedReason: Transformers.js registers no InternVL model class. Verified 2026-07-19: grepping the shared pinned build @huggingface/transformers@3.7.5 AND the latest @4.2.0 dist/transformers.web.js (and src/tokenizers.js) finds no InternVL* class, no image-text-to-text auto-map for model_type "internvl" (only substrings "Internal"/"internally"); the config of the only browser ONNX exports (third-party BricksDisplay/InternVL3-1B-onnx / -2B-onnx) declares model_type "internvl", architecture "InternVLForConditionalGeneration" — so a dedicated-class or pipeline load throws "Unsupported model type: internvl" (same failure class as pegasus/gliner/got-ocr2). No onnx-community InternVL repo exists (search + resolve of onnx-community/InternVL2_5-1B-MPO/-ONNX and onnx-community/InternVL3-1B-ONNX all 401). The upstream OpenGVLab/InternVL2_5-1B-MPO is safetensors-only. Unblocks when Transformers.js adds an InternVL model class + processor (dynamic tiling, pixel-shuffle, interleaved image tokens) and a canonical onnx-community ONNX export ships.

models.name: AI-generated image detector (Swin)
models.blurb: Screen an image locally for whether it looks AI-generated or a real photograph — an AI-generated vs real probability with the confidence behind it, framed honestly as an imperfect signal, not proof of origin.
models.unlocks: Private, offline provenance triage: route likely-AI uploads to human review with no API bill and no image ever leaving the device.

support.agentNote: Agent matrix pass 2026-07-19: real inference confirmed (cats photo → REAL PHOTO 100%, WASM 523ms); auto-init on cached reload (no Download button); 0 console errors first-visit + cached; light+dark legible (WCAG-AA design tokens, no raw hex); mobile 360×740 + desktop 1280×800 no horizontal overflow. Sub-24px items are inline text links (crumbs/references/footer) + the range slider — acceptable, primary controls (dropzone, sample thumbs, buttons) ≥44px.

models.name: Model2Vec static embeddings (potion-base-8M)
models.blurb: Turn text into vectors with a distilled lookup table — no transformer, no attention, just gather-and-average. Microsecond-scale embeddings on a plain CPU for semantic search and clustering.
models.unlocks: Blazing-fast, tiny-footprint embeddings: index a huge corpus or re-embed on every keystroke on-device, where an attention model like MiniLM would be too slow.

support.agentNote: Agent matrix pass 2026-07-19: real inference confirmed (6-sentence cosine matrix with correct semantics — cat/kitten 0.45, stock/shares 0.53, unrelated ~0; 256-d; WASM 12ms batch); auto-init on cached reload (no Download button); 0 console errors first-visit + cached (ORT graph-optimizer notices silenced via logLevel); light+dark legible; mobile + desktop no horizontal overflow. Sub-24px items are inline text links + range/number inputs — acceptable, primary controls ≥44px.

models.name: Mask2Former (universal segmentation)
models.blurb: Universal segmentation — one masked-attention architecture for semantic, instance AND panoptic — distinct from SegFormer (semantic) and DETR-panoptic.
models.unlocks: A single model for every segmentation task via mask classification instead of per-pixel labels.
models.blockedReason: No transformers.js model class exists for Mask2Former in ANY version. Verified 2026-07-19: the MODEL_FOR_UNIVERSAL_SEGMENTATION_MAPPING registers only ['detr','DetrForSegmentation'] and ['maskformer','MaskFormerForInstanceSegmentation'] in both the shared 3.7.5 pin AND the newest 4.2.0 (latest) — only a Mask2FormerImageProcessor ships, no Mask2FormerModel/Mask2FormerForUniversalSegmentation, so loading model_type 'mask2former' fails with 'Unsupported model type: mask2former'. Separately, NO browser ONNX export exists to load even if the class did: the canonical facebook/mask2former-* repos are safetensors/PyTorch only (0 .onnx files; config model_type confirmed 'mask2former'), and neither Xenova nor onnx-community publish any mask2former ONNX repo (Xenova/mask2former-swin-tiny-coco-panoptic does not exist → HF 404). The version-pin escape hatch (as used for SAM2's Sam2Model in 4.2.0) does not help because 4.2.0 also lacks the class. Not shipped rather than mislabel MaskFormer (a different architecture, which IS runnable) as Mask2Former. Unblocks when transformers.js registers a Mask2Former universal-segmentation model class AND an ONNX export (via Xenova/onnx-community) ships.

models.name: Snowflake Arctic-Embed-S (retrieval embeddings)
models.blurb: Snowflake retrieval-tuned embedder — CLS pooling with an asymmetric query prefix, 384-d vectors.
models.unlocks: First-stage retrieval and private RAG in the tab, with the query-prefix trick shown A/B and Arctic/GTE/MiniLM compared.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass 2026-07-19: headless Chrome real Download(~34MB)+embed — prefixed query ranked correct passage #1 (cosine 0.653), 384-d CLS vectors, live A/B prefix shift; desktop 1280 light+dark + mobile 360x740 DPR3 screenshots reviewed — 0px overflow, legible, operable controls, 0 console errors.

models.name: Grammar correction (grammar-synthesis-small)
models.blurb: A T5 seq2seq fine-tuned to rewrite ungrammatical, typo-ridden, or ESL English into clean prose, with a word-level diff.
models.unlocks: A private, offline proofreader — regenerates the whole sentence, robust to ASR transcripts and heavy errors; no API key or cloud.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass 2026-07-19: headless Chrome real Download(~95MB)+correct — distinct real seq2seq corrections with a word-level diff, streamed token-by-token; desktop 1280 light+dark + mobile 360x740 DPR3 screenshots reviewed — 0px overflow, legible, operable controls, 0 console errors.

models.name: GTE-large (1024-d retrieval embeddings)
models.blurb: The heavyweight GTE embedder: a BERT-large encoder that emits 1024-d sentence vectors with higher MTEB retrieval quality than GTE-small — same mean-pool, no-prefix recipe, a much larger download.
models.unlocks: Server-grade retrieval quality entirely client-side: sharper semantic search, near-duplicate detection and RAG shortlists where a mis-ranked hit is expensive — pick this tier when accuracy beats the ~337 MB/latency cost.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass (headless Chrome, real 337 MB download + on-device run): desktop 1280x800 light+dark and mobile 360x740 DPR3 dark screenshots reviewed — no horizontal overflow (0px on mobile), at-a-glance table wraps, legible text, controls operable, console clean. Real output: 1024-d embeddings, 6x6 cosine matrix, semantic search ranked the credentials line #1 for 'I can't sign in' (675 ms). Cached reload auto-inits to ready with no Download button.

models.name: Question generation (text2text seq2seq)
models.blurb: Given a passage (and optionally a highlighted answer span), generate reading-comprehension questions — a distinct text2text use from summarize/translate.
models.unlocks: Auto-generated quizzes, flashcards and study aids, and Socratic question chains — from any passage, on-device.
models.blockedReason: No dedicated question-generation model ships a Transformers.js-3.7.5-loadable ONNX that produces usable output (verified 2026-07-19). (1) The classic QG checkpoints have NO ONNX export at all: valhalla/t5-small-qg-hl, valhalla/t5-small-e2e-qg, mrm8488/t5-base-finetuned-question-generation-ap, iarfmoose/t5-base-question-generator, and the lmqg/*, p208p2002/*, allenai and research-backup QG families are PyTorch/safetensors-only (0 .onnx files each). (2) The only dedicated QG model with a v3-loadable merged-decoder ONNX is dantedgp/flan-t5-small-finetuned-question-generation, and its export is DEGENERATE: it ships ONLY quantized ONNX (no fp32 fallback) and every decoding config (greedy, beam x4, sampling, repetition_penalty/no_repeat_ngram) returns repeated-token garbage ('The The The...', 'About About About...'); the identical harness on Xenova/flan-t5-small q8 returns real questions, proving the harness is correct and the dantedgp quantized weights are broken. (3) A genuine -qg-hl model with the <hl>/<sep> answer-span mechanism exists in ONNX — WiseIntelligence/t5-base-question-generator-Optimum-ONNX (and its -Quantized-AVX2 sibling, ~422 MB) — but both use the pre-v3 SEPARATE-decoder layout (decoder_model.onnx + decoder_with_past_model.onnx, no decoder_model_merged.onnx). Transformers.js 3.7.5 hardcodes decoder_model_merged for T5ForConditionalGeneration generation (verified in dist source: sessions['decoder_model_merged']), so it errors 'Could not locate decoder_model_merged.onnx' and cannot load them. Relabeling the already-built Xenova/flan-t5-small instruction demo as a dedicated QG model is disallowed. Unblocks when a real QG model ships a Transformers.js-v3-loadable (decoder_model_merged) ONNX with non-degenerate output — e.g. a Xenova/onnx-community re-export of a t5-qg-hl checkpoint.

models.name: Age estimation
models.blurb: Estimate APPARENT age on-device across 9 buckets, folded into one expected-age number — an imperfect, bias-carrying signal, never identity or verification.
models.unlocks: Private, consented age-aware UX in the browser: signal-not-verdict defaults, faceless audience analytics, and calibration-gated decisions — with the frame never leaving the device.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: real headless inference (sample face → expected ≈31 yrs, correct 9-bucket softmax, WASM, 0 console errors); cached reload auto-inits with no Load button; desktop+mobile responsive-check clean (no horizontal overflow); light+dark screenshots read (good contrast, controls stack/keyboard-operable). Camera path degrades honestly when getUserMedia is unavailable.

models.name: Paraphrase generation (T5)
models.blurb: Rewrite a sentence while preserving meaning — a dedicated T5-base paraphraser that produces several diverse re-wordings via sampling, side-by-side with similarity read-outs.
models.unlocks: A local rewrite engine: copywriting A/B variants, editor reword buttons, and dataset augmentation — no API key, no per-call bill, no text leaving the device.

support.evidence: Agent matrix pass: real headless inference (genuine meaning-preserving paraphrases, sampling variety across runs, WASM, 0 console errors); cached reload auto-inits with no Load button after ~235MB download; desktop+mobile responsive-check clean; light+dark screenshots read (cards reflow to one column on mobile, good contrast).

models.name: TAPAS (table question answering)
models.blurb: TAPAS answers natural-language questions over a TABLE — selecting cells and applying an aggregation (SUM/COUNT/AVERAGE) — a structured-data QA task distinct from free-text extractive QA. Not runnable in the browser today — see blockedReason.
models.unlocks: Spreadsheet / receipt / report Q&A on-device (ask a table a question, get an answer + the cells + the aggregation) — if it ran in the browser. Blocked by a missing model class AND a missing pipeline task AND a missing ONNX export, not a license gap.
models.blockedReason: TAPAS does not run via the shared Transformers.js path today (verified 2026-07-19), for three independent reasons. (1) NO tapas model class is registered in Transformers.js 3.7.5 (webai.js TRANSFORMERS_URL pin) OR 4.2.0 (latest): grepping every dist bundle (transformers.js / .min.js / .web.js / .node.cjs) for 'tapas', 'TapasFor', 'TapasForQuestionAnswering' returns ZERO matches in both versions, so loading would throw 'Unsupported model type: tapas'. (2) The 'table-question-answering' pipeline task does not exist in Transformers.js 3.7.5 or 4.2.0 — grep of the bundles for 'table-question-answering' returns zero matches (the pipeline registry has question-answering, text-classification, token-classification, zero-shot-classification, etc., but no table variant); pipeline('table-question-answering', ...) → 'Unsupported pipeline'. (3) NO browser ONNX export exists: Xenova/tapas-base-finetuned-wtq → HTTP 401/absent, onnx-community/tapas-base-finetuned-wtq → HTTP 401/absent, Xenova/tapas-base → 401; only google/tapas-base-finetuned-wtq exists and it is PyTorch/safetensors-only with no onnx/ folder. TAPAS also needs its own TapasTokenizer/TapasForQuestionAnswering table encoding (row/column/segment/rank embeddings + a cell-selection + aggregation head) that Transformers.js has no processor for. Running it would require a bespoke ONNX Runtime Web pipeline plus a hand-written TAPAS table tokenizer off the shared createModelLoader path. Recheck when a TAPAS ONNX export ships AND Transformers.js adds a tapas class + table-question-answering pipeline. Do NOT relabel a free-text extractive QA model (e.g. DistilBERT SQuAD) as table QA.

models.name: T5-base (task-prefix seq2seq)
models.blurb: The ORIGINAL T5: one text-to-text model whose task is chosen entirely by an explicit prefix — translate English to German:, summarize:, cola sentence: (grammaticality), stsb sentence1:… sentence2:… (similarity). Change the prefix and the same weights do a different job.
models.unlocks: A single 250 MB download that translates, summarises, grammar-checks, and scores sentence similarity on-device — and a legible demo of the multi-task-via-prefix format that underlies modern LLMs.

models.license: unlicensed (HuggingFace CodeBERTa; CodeSearchNet data MIT; weights public + ungated)
models.blurb: A masked language model from Hugging Face’s CodeBERT family, pre-trained on source CODE (CodeSearchNet: Go, Java, JavaScript, PHP, Python, Ruby). Put a <mask> in a snippet and it predicts the missing code token with the exact logits over its 52k code vocabulary.
models.unlocks: Offline, bidirectional code-token prediction — in-editor identifier/API-name hints, naming help, and anomaly signals — with no server and no prompt engineering.

models.name: AIMv2 (self-supervised image features)
models.blurb: Apple's AIMv2 vision encoder — self-supervised image features from AUTOREGRESSIVE multimodal pretraining (predict image patches + text), a distinct SSL recipe from DINOv2's discriminative self-distillation or CLIP's contrastive image-text training.
models.unlocks: Would add an autoregressive-multimodal-pretrained SSL feature extractor alongside the built DINOv2 / DINOv2-registers / CLIP-image demos — image embeddings, patch similarity, and image-image retrieval.
models.blockedReason: AIMv2 is not browser-runnable via transformers.js today. Verified 2026-07-19: (1) transformers.js registers NO AIMv2 model class — grep of the 3.7.5 AND 4.2.0 (latest published) dist bundles for aimv2 / aimv2_vision_model / Aimv2 / AIMv2Model returns ZERO matches. (2) Real load attempt with transformers.js 4.2.0: pipeline('image-feature-extraction','apple/aimv2-large-patch14-224') emits '[resolve_model_type] Architecture(s) not found in MODEL_TYPE_MAPPING: [Aimv2VisionModel] for model type aimv2_vision_model. Falling back to EncoderOnly' and then fails 'Could not locate file: .../onnx/model.onnx' — the official apple/aimv2-* repos are safetensors + custom_code (auto_map -> modeling_aimv2.py, which transformers.js cannot trust_remote_code) with NO onnx/ export and a restrictive apple-amlr license. (3) The only community ONNX, neody/aimv2-large-patch14-336-distilled-onnx, ships a bare model.onnx with NO config.json ('Could not locate .../config.json'), so the pipeline cannot resolve or preprocess it; other community repos (lucasjin/*, amaye15/*) carry config.json (model_type aimv2) but NO onnx files. No single repo combines a loadable onnx/+config layout with a transformers.js-registered class. Not shipped rather than relabel the built DINOv2 / DINOv2-with-registers / CLIP-image demos as AIMv2 (a materially different SSL pretraining objective). Unblocks if transformers.js adds an aimv2 model class (as it did for qwen2_5_vl/sam2 in 4.x) AND an onnx-community browser build with a proper config.json lands, or the showcase adds an onnxruntime-web direct runtime that can drive the bare ONNX with a hand-written processor.
